card.hook: A browser-side transaction firewall only holds if it combines three checks before wallet approval: opt-in tab-scoped inspection, origin/scam-risk signals, and independent smart-contract preflight plus verification. Drop any one of those pieces and a clean-looking site can still push a user toward signing an opaque or harmful contract call.
card.why_it_matters: If you are learning to build browser-extension security for smart-contract flows, this gives you a concrete architecture for enforcing checks before the wallet becomes the first line of defense.
card.action: Write a 3-step decision table in a local note or README with rows for origin risk, contract verification status, and eth_call/eth_estimateGas preflight result, and add a final allow/block rule for each failure case described in the article.
